comments.text: The page should be updated to include the later result of Godsil and McKay (1990). If $L_{k,n}$ denotes the unrestricted number of $k\times n$ Latin rectangles on symbols $[n]$, they proved that, as $n\to\infty$ with $k=o(n^{6/7})$, $$L_{k,n}\sim (n!)^k\left(\frac{(n)_k}{n^k}\right)^n\left(1-\frac{k}{n}\right)^{-n/2}e^{-k/2},$$ where $(n)_k=n(n-1)\cdots(n-k+1)$. This substantially extends the earlier results of Erdos-Kaplansky and Yamamoto. It's probably also worth clarifying that OEIS A001009 is not the unrestricted count $L_{k,n}$. It records the reduced/normalised counts $R_{k,n}$ (ie. with the first row and first column fixed).
